name: Team Kaine
subject: Supporting survivors of campus sexual assault
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---

Derek,

Last week, Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos announced upcoming changes to key protections for sexual assault survivors on college campuses. Here's what happened:

In 2011, the Department of Education released a letter to guide college campuses through handling sexual violence issues in adherence to Title IX. The letter not only called out the right -- ensured by the Supreme Court -- for all students to have a learning environment free of sexual violence, but also represented a promise that the government would enforce that right. Now, the Trump Administration may walk that promise back.

**[Add your name if you agree: The decision to change the way we handle campus sexual assault issues must not be taken lightly -- and must, above all else, allow for fair and thoughtful investigations that support survivors.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=YXYXHjh1yoKOyqfzJzHE%2Bf7ykEexN7hq)**

Sexual violence on college campuses is an epidemic in our country. The prevalence of assault is hard to accurately measure, and many survivors may not feel able to speak out or report their experiences. Studies suggest that nearly 1 in 5 women has been sexually assaulted during their time at school, and more than 40% of dating college women report experiencing violent and abusive dating behaviors. This is simply inexcusable -- and while we work to stop this culture of widespread violence, we also must be dedicated to supporting survivors at the administrative and legislative levels.

That's why Tim has introduced legislation to require all campuses to have a survivor advocate, helping to ensure that students can feel safe and get access to the resources they need. And it's why Betsy DeVos must think twice before rolling back key protections that the Obama Administration established.

**Sign your name today to say you agree that we should be doing more, not less, to combat campus sexual assault:**

**[http://timkaine.com/support-survivors](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=ZjFejugjYQ00lhFdR8f4I%2F7ykEexN7hq)**

Thank you.

\- Team Kaine
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=m42tBfhHTCgCzQbr2V%2Bfff7ykEexN7hq)

name: Tom Perez
subject: President Obama's coming - are you?
body:
Enter for the chance to meet Barack Obama later this month.
---
**You and a friend could meet President Obama in Washington, D.C. Pitch in $3 or more and you'll be automatically entered for the chance to win!
**[ENTER NOW](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523536/4067b0a/3f95c5765/7e61bd70/1028943529/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Meet-President-Obama-Contest-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ENTER+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20170915_tp_nd&source=em_20170915_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170915_tp_nd")**
**
Derek --

Over the past few months, I've been crisscrossing the country to meet with Democrats like you, talk about why it's so important that we keep fighting for the values we believe in, and share our strategy for winning elections at every level.

There's another one of these conversations coming up in a couple of weeks in Washington, D.C., and I just got some exciting news: President Obama has RSVPed!

So we're holding a spot open for a grassroots supporter like you, so you can meet President Obama and talk about why being a Democrat is so important to you.

Chip in $3 or whatever you can to be automatically entered for a chance to win a free trip to Washington to meet President Obama -- airfare and hotel are on us.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523536/4067b0a/3f95c5765/7e61bd07/1028943529/VEsHBw/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170915_tp_nd&source=em_20170915_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170915_tp_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work President Obama and other Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523536/4067b0a/3f95c5765/7e61bd04/1028943529/VEsHBg/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=13+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170915_tp_nd&source=em_20170915_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170915_tp_nd"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523536/4067b0a/3f95c5765/7e61bd02/1028943529/VEsHAA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=15+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170915_tp_nd&source=em_20170915_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170915_tp_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting President Obama and other Democrats.

subject: as bad as we expected (DeVos)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | | Peter,

**Back in February, Joe voted against confirming Betsy DeVos as Secretary of Education because he knew she'd be a disaster for our schools and our kids.**

But thanks to Senate Republicans, she was confirmed anyway -- and she's been as bad as we expected. She's proposed devastating cuts to our education system, tried to hand money meant for public schools over to private corporations, and worked to end programs set up to provide student loan debt relief.

**Now, she's even announced plans to roll back critical Title IX protections for victims of sexual assault.**

That's why we want to know how you think she's doing, Peter. We need 157 more responses to meet our goal of 500.

**How would you rate Betsy DeVos' performance as Education Secretary?**

clean_body: | | | | Peter, Back in February, Joe voted against confirming Betsy DeVos as Secretary of Education because he knew she'd be a disaster for our schools and our kids. But thanks to Senate Republicans, she was confirmed anyway -- and she's been as bad as we expected. She's proposed devastating cuts to our education system, tried to hand money meant for public schools over to private corporations, and worked to end programs set up to provide student loan debt relief. Now, she's even announced plans to roll back critical Title IX protections for victims of sexual assault. That's why we want to know how you think she's doing, Peter. We need 157 more responses to meet our goal of 500. How would you rate Betsy DeVos' performance as Education Secretary? | "" | "" | "" | "" \-- Team Donnelly | "" | Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana P.O. Box 891, Indianapolis, IN 46206 ""

subject: ICYMI - Congress must fix rigged tax code
body:
**Derek,**
**In a recent letter I submitted to the Union Leader, I wrote:
**
_Our tax code is broken. The system is rigged. And for too long, as a country, we have accepted this as the status quo._
_For Granite Staters who work demanding hours, the size of Uncle Sam’s cut of your paycheck should be deeply disturbing. In order to give working-class Americans a pay raise, we must prioritize comprehensive tax reform that lowers and simplifies taxes._
_Ninety-six percent of employers in New Hampshire are considered small businesses. Not only is our current tax code a burden for individuals and families, but also for our employers and businesses._
[Read the rest of my letter on tax reform here.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbY1qiyT3HuRDH6pVWPAnYR68FCZ1ORcmaqVtkCxJzc4U2tUjVXk66RDOKg0bJvWL-2FU4-2BKmyBrbO-2FDlV2vN4qdEXzohurw8B49Krl9aRjuv9ysyvbxS2t2BYxZPI1oMCzHQ-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MWvSZTu8DSbwW-2BtV0Ii-2FkNxXK1LDcUBHKnTTJNc8-2F8qfTLQK3Uxr-2B7eHDwKES-2BBlcwrmPGdmBe7y8NWBl2eMXW7VaIyEpZeQLHpHDsNRyKuaR269Mw-2FA-2FIk37AbiKa07-2Fo-2Bl2gcUQJXz2-2FJHHHP0lKdDKzH6XMf6azDkKcTBiv-2B36NJ1-2F2R6e2L4Q0PVFwMYdwrh6zl7U6JMZhpP9n9wwDuH1E3a8iSmwaZjtzJOzc4-2BRAwXQlF2hq-2FNciuIrzFiBZy6wWwxI9RWyX4cVU22ElQ-3D-3D) [ ** Click here to tweet about my message on tax reform!** ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyEKihPlKbay24gpdxStR2Zg-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MWvSZTu8DSbwW-2BtV0Ii-2FkNxXK1LDcUBHKnTTJNc8-2F8qfTLQK3Uxr-2B7eHDwKES-2BBlcwrmPGdmBe7y8NWBl2eMXW6hXHYo0Svp0LxZxPdZdu91ZRPy7Hd8ot66oH2NvYjglcYy4As9TyawMTi2NrjUrLx6rl9mwdJNm-2BWexocpqlBseSctjX94iHQx-2B2x5r3pRQl2MJQc-2FbUXoN-2Ba4XUdK5sli07BTraFCkFcR32ExuetkdaHQ4529lZyGNrKY4pcim8HwX-2B-2BeFb-2BkBT1dXarosQg-3D-3D) Please help us share the message of my latest letter by sharing it with your friends so we can make the biggest social media impact possible!

**America's Heroes **
* * *
Last Saturday, I went to Fire Ops 101. No, I’m not changing jobs. But I was given the opportunity to experience something unlike anything I’ve ever experienced before thanks to the Professional Firefighters Union of Indiana and the team at Anderson Fire Fighters Local 1262. For several hours, I was put through my paces on climbing fire ladders, hauling hoses, performing CPR and car extrications and, the real test, crawling through the flashover chamber where we experienced a real fire while in full gear and on air with temperatures in the 500 degree range. When they told me I would feel the heat, they weren’t kidding! And while I knew I would come out alive and ok when it was all said and done, the firefighters who actually work to protect us every day never have that assurance. Neither do their brothers and sisters responding to the natural disasters that have recently come to Texas and Florida, or those who were the first to arrive at Ground Zero on the tragic morning of September 11, 2001. My appreciation for what they do, and the risks they take, has new meaning after last weekend. We are blessed to have among us public servants who willingly go into harm’s way to help their fellow neighbor – here at home and across the world. \- Kyle Hupfer

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer with **Rob Williams** (right) and **Tom Hanify** (left), president of the Professional Firefighters Union of Indiana.)
|

| This week Governor **Eric Holcomb** made international [headlines](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1e4d0d6b0c&e=1acd9bb3d6) with his visit to Japan. He visited Indiana's Japanese sister state, [Tochigi Prefecture](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=eed4882c17&e=1acd9bb3d6), attended the 49th Annual Japan-U.S. Midwest Association Joint Meeting alongside other U.S. governors, and [met with](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1c59d6d35d&e=1acd9bb3d6) leadership of the Japanese External Trade Organization. Indiana and Japan share a strong economic relationship with [nearly 60,000 Hoosiers](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6cdf667fc3&e=1acd9bb3d6) working for Japanese-based companies located here in Indiana. Our nationally ranked business climate, low cost of living, and healthy state budget are just a few attractions that have brought investments from overseas to our state. As Governor Holcomb said, “We want them to know just how much we value our friendship and our partnership and what it means, not just what it means to the people and their jobs and the opportunities, but their corporate citizenship as well.”

Indiana has proven what cutting taxes can do for a state’s economy, now it is time to do it on the national level. For years, Indiana has continued to close the doors on stifling tax rates and in return has opened the doors for businesses across state lines as well as those overseas to set up show in one of the most tax friendly states in the nation.

Let the White House know that YOU support taking this proven success story nationwide with the passage of federal tax form by signing the petition at the link above. It's time.

clean_body: | | | | | | America's Heroes Last Saturday, I went to Fire Ops 101. No, I’m not changing jobs. But I was given the opportunity to experience something unlike anything I’ve ever experienced before thanks to the Professional Firefighters Union of Indiana and the team at Anderson Fire Fighters Local 1262. For several hours, I was put through my paces on climbing fire ladders, hauling hoses, performing CPR and car extrications and, the real test, crawling through the flashover chamber where we experienced a real fire while in full gear and on air with temperatures in the 500 degree range. When they told me I would feel the heat, they weren’t kidding! And while I knew I would come out alive and ok when it was all said and done, the firefighters who actually work to protect us every day never have that assurance. Neither do their brothers and sisters responding to the natural disasters that have recently come to Texas and Florida, or those who were the first to arrive at Ground Zero on the tragic morning of September 11, 2001. My appreciation for what they do, and the risks they take, has new meaning after last weekend. We are blessed to have among us public servants who willingly go into harm’s way to help their fellow neighbor – here at home and across the world. \- Kyle Hupfer (Photo: Chairman Hupfer with Rob Williams (right) and Tom Hanify (left), president of the Professional Firefighters Union of Indiana.) | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | This week Governor Eric Holcomb made international with his visit to Japan. He visited Indiana's Japanese sister state, , attended the 49th Annual Japan-U.S. Midwest Association Joint Meeting alongside other U.S. governors, and leadership of the Japanese External Trade Organization. Indiana and Japan share a strong economic relationship with working for Japanese-based companies located here in Indiana. Our nationally ranked business climate, low cost of living, and healthy state budget are just a few attractions that have brought investments from overseas to our state. As Governor Holcomb said, “We want them to know just how much we value our friendship and our partnership and what it means, not just what it means to the people and their jobs and the opportunities, but their corporate citizenship as well.” | | The time for tax reform is NOW. Indiana has proven what cutting taxes can do for a state’s economy, now it is time to do it on the national level. For years, Indiana has continued to close the doors on stifling tax rates and in return has opened the doors for businesses across state lines as well as those overseas to set up show in one of the most tax friendly states in the nation. **NH REPUBLICAN PARTY****WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 15TH** **_A Note From Jeanie_** **Dear Derek,** **The last few weeks have been amazing and I wanted to share my observations. Republicans supporting Republicans...it's a very good thing!**
****
**Senator Boutin had a lot of volunteer support in his race and when the race was over, he didn't stop. He is still campaigning, but for other candidates. He's been knocking on doors, making calls, and helping raise money for other folks who are running. **
****
**Whether it was for Vincent Migliore in Grafton 9 or Steve Whalley in Belknap 9, he was there for these candidates. Likewise Vincent helped in Sen. Boutin's race and in Steve Whalley's race.**
****
**The other night we attended a meet and greet hosted by Rep. Jess Edwards for Jim Headd who is running in the Rockingham 4 District. Governor Sununu, Speaker Jasper, and Majority Leader Hinch, plus Senators Carson and Gannon and Representatives Edwards and Ammon also attended the event. It was wonderful to see so much support for Jim.**
****
**I've seen former NHGOP Vice Chair Matt Mayberry out there for every single Special Election to date. NH State Senators and Representatives have been helping including Senators Carson, French, Giuda, Morse, Gannon, and Representatives Johnson, Dean-Bailey, Sylvia, Cordelli, Varney, Marsh, Schmidt, and many more.**
****
**The Special Election candidates have been receiving financial support as well, whether through Senate and House PACs, the local GOP committees, individual Senator and Representative personal donations...the support has been there.**
****
**These folks get it...we need everyone working together and coming together to get our Special Election candidates across the finish line. **
****
**We CANNOT let our majorities slip away in the House and Senate, or else Governor Sununu, one of the most popular and effective Governors in the country, will not have the support he needs to keep making positive movement forward.**
****
**So this is a "shout out" to all those Republicans who are helping Republicans get a elected. Thank you!!**
**And it is a call to action to you, who are receiving this message. Even it is not in your district, won't you help us get these candidates across the finish line? We need your help as soon as you can give it! We have 4 more elections coming up, in Rockingham 4, Strafford 13, Sullivan 1, and Hillsborough 15 through ****November. Please email [Tara](mailto:tara@nhgop.org "tara@nhgop.org") or [Joe](mailto:joe@nhgop.org "joe@nhgop.org") ****and say "Yes, I want to help!" Your financial gift or the gift of your time will make the difference. **
**We have gained the majority and we must keep our majority and we do that by winning elections--when Republicans vote, Republicans win.**
**Sincerely, **
**Jeanie**
_**P.S. Congratulations to Vincent Migliore who ran in the Grafton 9 Special Election and prevailed in his recount. Thank you to Steve Whalley for stepping up to run in Belknap 09. **_ **Join the Grassroots Circle!**

**The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party.**
**Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor.** [ **More Information** ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurnZ5zJfDwV5-2F8Hl4xre-2BIMt5nd0pHim8EqEA9pV9ZCkm_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MbVmOl-2FhveG-2FOr9pJ1xC-2FIouWG5U8YgQ-2BDgrLmd07rWz01myC57WXFCfMhkQOu8jIzGUo2GtRmsIWSYUvWZf5o1iHBf9W3DcHmd428BsW1y5tnMjBfoo8161VLBBGAjXB5qQXqFGxe-2Bh91r-2Ba21sTFbpDpoqznlnAOB7XO5zU-2BWGYU5Mltte6BiaojShfAkJWaavnLvApYjYyGUM18OEYfYIT1YBZ-2Bz6JE6xAWA0YKbXgsBfQbQXTLHmVYJUUgDqhX6Iz-2FBZ4WfDCIyLWVuYZfA-3D-3D) **Intern with the NH GOP!**

clean_body: nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTYWEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 15TH A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, The last few weeks have been amazing and I wanted to share my observations. Republicans supporting Republicans...it's a very good thing! ** Senator Boutin had a lot of volunteer support in his race and when the race was over, he didn't stop. He is still campaigning, but for other candidates. He's been knocking on doors, making calls, and helping raise money for other folks who are running. Whether it was for Vincent Migliore in Grafton 9 or Steve Whalley in Belknap 9, he was there for these candidates. Likewise Vincent helped in Sen. Boutin's race and in Steve Whalley's race. The other night we attended a meet and greet hosted by Rep. Jess Edwards for Jim Headd who is running in the Rockingham 4 District. Governor Sununu, Speaker Jasper, and Majority Leader Hinch, plus Senators Carson and Gannon and Representatives Edwards and Ammon also attended the event. It was wonderful to see so much support for Jim. I've seen former NHGOP Vice Chair Matt Mayberry out there for every single Special Election to date. NH State Senators and Representatives have been helping including Senators Carson, French, Giuda, Morse, Gannon, and Representatives Johnson, Dean-Bailey, Sylvia, Cordelli, Varney, Marsh, Schmidt, and many more. The Special Election candidates have been receiving financial support as well, whether through Senate and House PACs, the local GOP committees, individual Senator and Representative personal donations...the support has been there. These folks get it...we need everyone working together and coming together to get our Special Election candidates across the finish line. We CANNOT let our majorities slip away in the House and Senate, or else Governor Sununu, one of the most popular and effective Governors in the country, will not have the support he needs to keep making positive movement forward. So this is a "shout out" to all those Republicans who are helping Republicans get a elected. Thank you!! And it is a call to action to you, who are receiving this message. Even it is not in your district, won't you help us get these candidates across the finish line? We need your help as soon as you can give it! We have 4 more elections coming up, in Rockingham 4, Strafford 13, Sullivan 1, and Hillsborough 15 through **November. Please email mailto:tara@nhgop.org "tara@nhgop.org" or mailto:joe@nhgop.org "joe@nhgop.org" and say "Yes, I want to help!" Your financial gift or the gift of your time will make the difference. We have gained the majority and we must keep our majority and we do that by winning elections--when Republicans vote, Republicans win. Sincerely, Jeanie P.S. Congratulations to Vincent Migliore who ran in the Grafton 9 Special Election and prevailed in his recount. Thank you to Steve Whalley for stepping up to run in Belknap 09. Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics?** Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. subject: Two weeks

body:
****
**** **** | |
| | **September 15, 2017**
---|---

When President Ronald Reagan signed a new tax code into law, he had a clear vision: a pro-growth tax code that was fair and simple for American families. Since then, Washington special interests have taken control. Now, our tax code is ridiculously complicated, unfair to working families, and has driven jobs overseas.
__
Two weeks from now, that all ends. During the week of September 25th, Paul Ryan and House Republicans will roll out a tax code for the 21st century. We 'll help American businesses grow and create more jobs right here, shrink your tax form to the size of a postcard, and give a big tax break to middle-class families. Read about some of the other goals of tax reform here.

| **** | **The Wall Street Journal**
---
**Republicans Plan to Release Tax Overhaul Details Week of Sept. 25******

_
WASHINGTON --Republicans plan to show more details about their tax plans during the week of Sept. 25, as they prepare for a legislative sprint toward one of their top priorities.

"We will release a consensus document with the core elements of big, bold tax reform," Rep. Kevin Brady (R., Texas) told reporters Wednesday.

House Speaker Paul Ryan (R., Wis.) said narrow tax cuts like what Congress did in 2001 and 2003 won't fix the structural problems in the economy.

"We're weighing down our economy with a terrible tax system," he said in an interview with the Associated Press._

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
****
| **** | **The Weekly Standard**
---
**
Paul Ryan Plugs Away at Tax Reform**********

_
Despite a packed legislative calendar, House Speaker Paul Ryan says he 's still confident that Congress will have tax reform legislation on President Donald Trump's desk before the end of 2017.

"Our plan is to get this done at the end of the year for law, so that we start 2018 with a new tax system," Ryan said._

[](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| | **The Associated Press**
---
**Ryan: GOP Will Have Tax Overhaul By End of Year**

****

_House Speaker Paul Ryan said Congress has an 'aggressive timeline' for passing a tax reform bill, insisting it will be done by the end of the year. Ryan said tax reform is one of the GOP's "crown issues" and they "have to get this done." _

[**Watch More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| **** | **Reuters**
---
******House Speaker Ryan expects tax plan this fall******

_WASHINGTON (Reuters) - U.S. House of Representatives Speaker Paul Ryan said on Thursday he expects to unveil a proposal for overhauling the federal tax code this fall and to get legislation passed by the end of the year that includes a lower corporate tax rate.

"We want America to wake up on New Year's Day 2018 with a new tax system," Ryan said in an interview with The New York Times. He said congressional tax writers were close to releasing their template for the tax system overhaul.___

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| **** | **Forbes**
---
**2017 Is Our Year To Make History On Tax Reform**

_President Reagan put it best on the day he signed tax reform into law. "Fair and simpler for most Americans," he said, "this is a tax code designed to take us into a future of technological invention and economic achievement, one that will keep America competitive and growing into the 21st century."

Tax reform for the 21st century means rewarding hardworking families by closing unfair loopholes, lowering tax rates across the board, and simplifying the tax code dramatically. It demands reducing the tax burden on American businesses of all sizes so they can keep more of their income to invest in our communities. And, to unshackle productivity and wage growth in our nation, tax reform has to make America a magnet for innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic growth._
__

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MzA2MjUxMDkS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEyMTg1NjM3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: Doubling down on what we do best >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6010/24c69fd9/9c49650b/189abdf6/1855709595/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact&source=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact")
---

Peter,

"Now what?"

The question so many of us were asking ourselves after Election Day.

So in January, we marched. And at EMILY's List, we doubled down on what we do best -- electing pro-choice Democratic women.

We launched Run to Win, our largest ever investment in helping new candidates run for office. Almost _18,000_ women have told us they want to run, and I guarantee there are future mayors, state legislators, senators, and even a president among them.

Our team is crisscrossing the country training many of them and running our first-ever online trainings. But the time is quickly coming where we'll need to help many of these women pivot to actually launching their campaigns.

**[Donate $3 to help fund Run to Win so that we can support the wave of women who will change the face of government.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6010/24c69fd9/9c49650b/189abdf7/1855709595/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170915_email_HowWeResist?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+fund+Run+to+Win+so+that&utm_campaign=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact&source=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact")**

While Trump's policies threaten the rights of women, immigrants, LGBTQ Americans, and people of color in this country, EMILY's List's elected women are resisting too. Whether that's Rep. Maxine Waters vocalizing her strong opposition to Trump's agenda, Sen. Dianne Feinstein's pursuit of answers in the Russia investigations, or our dozens of state and local women passing laws to shield their communities from Trump's damage -- our women are pivotal to the resistance.

EMILY's List women continue to lead the charge every day. And you can count on them to get the job done.

That's why we're determined to elect even MORE of them to every level of government.

**Donate $3 to help elect more pro-choice Democratic women who will resist Trump and dangerous Republicans every step of the way:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/How-We-Resist](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6010/24c69fd9/9c49650b/189abdf0/1855709595/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170915_email_HowWeResist?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHowWeResist&utm_campaign=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact&source=WQEFE928N_digital_email_20170915_HowELResists_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

So in January, we marched. And at EMILY's List, we doubled down on what we do best -- electing pro-choice Democratic women. We launched Run to Win, our largest ever investment in helping new candidates run for office. Almost 18,000 women have told us they want to run, and I guarantee there are future mayors, state legislators, senators, and even a president among them. Our team is crisscrossing the country training many of them and running our first-ever online trainings. But the time is quickly coming where we'll need to help many of these women pivot to actually launching their campaigns.
